1.notes: Demanding par 4 opener. Allen Creek crosses in front of green. Fairway bunkers left at 250 yards.

2.notes: Downhill par 4. Bunkers guard both sides of landing area. Green slopes back to front.

3.notes: Long par 3 over Allen Creek. Deep bunkers front and back of green.

4.notes: Monster par 5. Fairway bunkers guard the corner. Allen Creek crosses twice. Risk/reward second shot.

5.notes: Uphill par 3 to elevated green. One of the toughest short holes on tour.

6.notes: Demanding par 4 with bunkers guarding landing area. Creek runs along left side.

7.notes: Uphill par 4 bending right. Strategic bunker placement. Famous 'Hill of Fame' hole.

8.notes: Downhill par 4 with Allen Creek left of green. Deep bunkers protect the putting surface.

9.notes: Demanding finishing hole on front nine. Uphill to well-protected green.

10.notes: Solid par 4 starting the back nine. Allen Creek crosses in front of green.

11.notes: Beautiful downhill par 3 over water. Green fronted by creek.

12.notes: Uphill par 4 with demanding second shot. Bunkers guard approach.

13.notes: Long par 5 bending left around trees. Allen Creek guards the approach to green.

14.notes: Short but demanding par 4. Uphill to well-bunkered green.

15.notes: Short par 3 but treacherous. Deep bunkers surround small green.

16.notes: Strong par 4 with demanding approach over Allen Creek.

17.notes: Long par 4 playing downhill. Strategic bunker placement guards landing area.

18.notes: Dramatic finishing hole. Uphill to amphitheater green. Allen Creek short of green.

1.notes: Demanding opener. Deep bunkers on both sides of fairway. Warning sign: 'Highly skilled golfers only'.

2.notes: Uphill par 4 with severe rough. Cross bunkers at 230 yards.

3.notes: Downhill par 3. Deep bunkers surround elevated green.

4.notes: Long par 5 bending right. Signature hole. Deep cross bunker at 280 yards.

5.notes: Demanding par 4 doglegging left. Tillinghast's classic design. Deep bunkers guard corner.

6.notes: Uphill par 4. Bunkers pinch the fairway at 250 yards.

7.notes: Monster par 4/5. One of the toughest holes in championship golf. Deep bunker complex left.

8.notes: Demanding uphill par 3. Deep bunkers front and back.

9.notes: Strong par 4 finishing front nine. Bunkers guard approach.

10.notes: Dramatic start to back nine. Sharp dogleg left. Deep bunker complex guards corner.

11.notes: Uphill par 4. Bunkers pinch fairway creating narrow landing zone.

12.notes: Long par 4 bending right. Deep bunkers on both sides of fairway.

13.notes: Monster par 5. One of the longest holes in major championship golf. Cross bunker at 310 yards.

14.notes: Short downhill par 3. Island green feel with deep bunkers surrounding.

15.notes: Brutal uphill par 4. Stroke index 1. Bunkers create very narrow fairway.

16.notes: Downhill par 4 bending left. Deep fairway bunkers on inside of dogleg.

17.notes: Stadium par 3. Famous grandstand hole. Deep bunkers surround elevated green.

18.notes: Strong finishing hole. Uphill par 4 to elevated green. Bunkers flank fairway and green.

1.notes: Opening par 4 with wide fairway. Sandy waste area borders right side. Classic turtleback green.

2.notes: Long par 4. Played as par 5 for women. Wide fairway with wire grass waste areas. Green runs away.

3.notes: Demanding par 4. Waste areas flank both sides. Small crowned green.

green.notes: Crowned green - balls reject off edges

4.notes: Long par 4 bending right. Bunkers guard inside of turn. Crowned green with steep falloff.

5.notes: Signature par 5. The longest hole on tour. Wide fairway with strategic bunkering. Water left of green.

6.notes: Uphill par 3. One of the most challenging short holes. Severely crowned green.

green.notes: Severely crowned - balls roll to collection areas

7.notes: Tough par 4 bending left. Bunker guards inside of turn. Turtleback green.

8.notes: Long par 4 played as par 5 for women. Uphill to crowned green. Challenging approach.

9.notes: Downhill par 3 finishing front nine. Green sits in natural bowl. Very difficult pin positions.

10.notes: Long par 5 starting back nine. Downhill tee shot. Crowned green with severe runoffs.

11.notes: Demanding uphill par 4. Bunkers guard approach. Classic Ross crowned green.

12.notes: Long par 4. Wide fairway with waste areas. Approach to severely crowned green.

13.notes: Shorter par 4 but very demanding. Crowned green rejects approaches.

14.notes: Long par 4. Stroke index 2. Demanding approach to crowned green.

15.notes: Short par 3. Small crowned green. Precision required.

16.notes: Par 4/5 with water left of green. Only water hazard on course. Risk/reward approach.

17.notes: Demanding par 3 playing uphill. Severely crowned green.

18.notes: Famous finishing hole. Uphill approach to crowned green. Payne Stewart statue behind green.

green.notes: Iconic finishing green - crowned with collection areas
